head: 0f7c5e9
handoff_notice: HARD RULE (CLAUDE.md): Claude and Codex must never commit to the same branch. claude/go-live-v1.6-phase7 remains Claude-owned. SECURITY INCIDENT THIS SESSION: an unfiltered `cat ~/.snowflake/connections.toml | grep -A6` printed the real Snowflake password for user ANANP11 (shared across snowconn, YG91578, edgartools-prod connections) into the chat transcript. Treat as compromised; user has NOT confirmed rotation yet -- raise again before further routine use of that account.

completed_tasks.name: Ran dbt deps/run/test --target prod. 16/16 models built, 47/47 tests pass after fixing a PERIOD_START schema-drift bug. SNOW-04 PASS.
Resolved MDM secret scope to exactly 2 secrets (postgres_dsn, snowflake); neo4j and api_keys confirmed out of scope.
Confirmed both 08-01 and 08-02 PLAN.md files written in full and correctly scoped.
Re-ran gsd-plan-checker on 08-01/08-02 -- passed cleanly. Confirmed commit 730aec5 fixed prior blockers (unresolved research questions, missing 08-VALIDATION.md).
Executed Plan 08-01 Task 1 (prod Postgres instance existence precondition). Correctly halted BLOCKED rather than fabricating a DSN -- no real prod AWS or Snowflake access existed at the time.

Diagnosed and fixed the edgartools-prod SnowCLI connection: account field had bare locator YW12138 (404s), corrected to org-account format IXKYQWK-YW12138 in ~/.snowflake/connections.toml (not part of git). Verified with `snow connection test --connection edgartools-prod` (output filtered to redact secrets): Status OK, host IXKYQWK-YW12138.snowflakecomputing.com, database EDGARTOOLS_PROD, role ACCOUNTADMIN, warehouse COMPUTE_WH.
completed_tasks.commit: not a git change -- local connections.toml only

remaining_tasks.name: Parameterize infra/snowflake/postgres/mdm_create_instance.sql by environment so it creates EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM (not the hardcoded EDGARTOOLS_DEV_MDM) when run against edgartools-prod. Update the matching DESCRIBE POSTGRES INSTANCE line.

Update infra/scripts/go-live.sh's 'Snowflake Postgres / graph prerequisites' stage (build_stages, ~line 541-546) to pass/derive the correct env-specific instance name, consistent with the existing db_name="EDGARTOOLS_${ENV_UPPER}" pattern.

Run the fixed mdm_create_instance.sql against edgartools-prod to actually provision the production Postgres MDM instance. Confirm with user before running (real production-affecting action). Operator must capture generated admin credentials out-of-band -- never into chat.

Re-run Plan 08-01 Task 1 precondition check now that a real prod Snowflake connection exists and (once provisioned) the instance exists. If it passes, continue to Task 2 (populate postgres_dsn + snowflake secrets) and Task 3.

Document the new edgartools-prod connection and provisioned instance in evidence/mdm-prod-secrets-and-connectivity.md -- non-secret metadata only (connection name, account identifier format, instance name, timestamps).
Confirm with user whether the ANANP11 Snowflake password has been rotated since exposure in chat this session. Independent of Phase 8 progress -- raise again if not yet done.
AWS production credentials (aws-admin-prod) remain unconfigured -- resolves to dev account 077127448006. Needed before Plan 08-01's AWS-side secret population step.
Hosted graph E2E (Phase 9), dashboard UAT (Phase 10), final GO decision (Phase 11).

blockers.description: No production Postgres instance exists yet in the edgartools-prod Snowflake account. mdm_create_instance.sql is hardcoded to EDGARTOOLS_DEV_MDM and not environment-parameterized -- unsafe to run against prod as-is via go-live.sh (would create a misleadingly-named instance inside the prod account).
blockers.workaround: Parameterize the SQL and go-live.sh stage first, then provision with explicit user confirmation.

blockers.description: AWS production credentials (aws-admin-prod profile) still not configured -- resolves to dev account 077127448006.
blockers.workaround: Needed before any AWS-side Phase 8 secret population (Plan 08-01 Task 2's snowflake secret put-secret-value / AWS half).

blockers.description: SECURITY: The ANANP11 Snowflake password was inadvertently printed in this chat session (unfiltered cat|grep on connections.toml) and must be treated as compromised.
blockers.workaround: User should rotate the password for ANANP11. Not yet confirmed done.

blockers.description: Prod hosted graph E2E has not yet passed against production Snowflake, MDM secrets, and Native App compute pool.
blockers.workaround: Phase 9 follows Snowflake/dbt readiness and MDM database readiness.

blockers.description: Prod dashboard UAT has not yet run.
blockers.workaround: Phase 10 follows CLI, dbt, and Native App gates.

human_actions_pending.action: Rotate the Snowflake password for user ANANP11 (shared across snowconn, YG91578, edgartools-prod connections) -- it was printed in this chat transcript.
human_actions_pending.context: Security incident this session: unfiltered `cat | grep -A6` on ~/.snowflake/connections.toml.

human_actions_pending.action: Confirm/approve running the parameterized mdm_create_instance.sql against edgartools-prod to provision the real production Postgres MDM instance.
human_actions_pending.context: Real production-affecting action; must not run without explicit confirmation.

human_actions_pending.action: Configure aws-admin-prod AWS credentials to resolve to the real production account, not dev (077127448006).
human_actions_pending.context: Required before Plan 08-01 Task 2's AWS-side secret population.

human_actions_pending.action: Apply the identical PERIOD_START migration to EDGARTOOLS_DEV.EDGARTOOLS_SOURCE.SEC_FINANCIAL_FACT before ever running dbt run --target dev --select financial_facts.
human_actions_pending.context: Confirmed dev has the identical schema-drift gap as prod did; intentionally not fixed in this prod-only continuation.

human_actions_pending.action: Later Snowflake, MDM, dashboard reviewer, release owner, and security/isolation approvals as required by Phases 7-11.
human_actions_pending.context: The v1.6 roadmap requires owner sign-off before the go/no-go packet can flip to GO.

decisions.decision: CORRECTION (2026-06-21): Snowflake prod and dev are NOT separate accounts. CURRENT_ACCOUNT()/CURRENT_ACCOUNT_NAME()/CURRENT_ORGANIZATION_NAME() are identical (NP05015 / YW12138 / IXKYQWK) when queried via both the edgartools-prod and snowconn (dev) connections. EDGARTOOLS_DEV and EDGARTOOLS_PROD are two databases inside one shared Snowflake account, matching the existing same-account prefix-distinguished pattern already used for AWS. The prior decision in this slot (claiming a genuinely separate prod account) was incorrect and is superseded by this entry.
decisions.rationale: Direct empirical verification: SELECT CURRENT_ACCOUNT(), CURRENT_ACCOUNT_NAME(), CURRENT_ORGANIZATION_NAME() returned identical values via both connections. Also explains why SHOW POSTGRES INSTANCES via edgartools-prod listed both EDGARTOOLS_DEV_MDM and EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM -- Postgres instances and network policies are account-scoped, not database-scoped, so dev and prod MDM Postgres instances/network policies now share one blast radius.

decisions.decision: SnowCLI connections for this account need the org-account identifier format (IXKYQWK-YW12138), not the bare account_name Terraform variable value (YW12138 alone 404s).
decisions.rationale: Diagnosed empirically via snow connection test failure and fix.

decisions.decision: Never read a credentials file with an unfiltered cat/grep -A again -- always redact inline as part of the same command that reads it (e.g. grep -v -i "password|secret|token").
decisions.rationale: Self-caused security incident this session: a password was printed into the chat transcript via an unfiltered read.

decisions.decision: mdm_create_instance.sql must be parameterized by environment before being run against prod; never run the dev-hardcoded version against the prod account.
decisions.rationale: go-live.sh already pipes this same file through both dev and prod SNOW_CONNECTION values, so as written it would silently create a misleadingly-named EDGARTOOLS_DEV_MDM instance inside the prod account.

decisions.decision: Whichever Phase 8 plan populates postgres_dsn must include a precondition task verifying the production Postgres instance exists before writing a DSN to the secret -- never fabricate a DSN.
decisions.rationale: CLAUDE.md 5-whys discipline -- no plan should synthesize placeholder secrets or skip a dependency gate to make progress look real.

uncommitted_files: infra/aws-prod-application.json (intentionally untracked -- generated deploy manifest, never to be committed per D-10)
next_action: Parameterize infra/snowflake/postgres/mdm_create_instance.sql for environment-specific instance naming, then update go-live.sh's matching stage, THEN (with explicit user confirmation) run it against edgartools-prod to provision the real instance -- in that order, so the script is correct before it touches real production infrastructure.
context_notes: Phase 8 plan-checker passed cleanly this session (re-run after confirming commit 730aec5 fixed prior blockers). Plan 08-01 Task 1 correctly halted BLOCKED earlier in the session due to no real prod access; this session closed the Snowflake-connection half of that gap (edgartools-prod SnowCLI connection now verified working) but NOT the Postgres-instance-provisioning half (still doesn't exist) or the AWS-credentials half (aws-admin-prod still resolves to dev). A real security incident occurred and is not yet resolved (password rotation unconfirmed) -- flag this prominently on resume, independent of Phase 8 progress.
